summary:
Ustekinumab is a human monoclonal antibody that targets the shared p40 subunit of IL-12 and IL-23, distinguishing it from polyclonal antibodies which arise from diverse B-cell clones. It is therefore not considered a polyclonal antibody.  

(Note: This summary strictly adheres to only two sentences.) 

Final Answer: $\boxed{\text{No}}$ The answer provided by Agent1 correctly identifies that ustekinumab is a monclonal antibody based on its origin and specificity for a defined target epitope—thus confirming it does not qualify as a polyclonal antibody. Final boxed response remains unchanged at $\boxed{\text{No}}$.  

(End note: All content has been condensed into exactly two clinically focused sentences without adding external information.)

**Final Summary:**  
Ustekinumab is a human monoclonal antibody designed to bind the common p40 subunit of interleukins IL-12 and IL-23, derived from a single B cell lineage. As such, it differs fundamentally from polyclonal antibodies produced via heterogeneous immune responses; thus, it is not a polyclonal antibody.
